groll_zeileis.name: Groll & Zeileis (TU Dortmund / Univ. Innsbruck) hybrid ML forecast
groll_zeileis.methodology: Random-forest hybrid blending four team-strength signals (bivariate-Poisson historic match abilities, 24-bookmaker consensus, plus-minus player ratings, Transfermarkt market values) + socio-economic features, trained on tournaments 2006-2024; 100,000 tournament simulations.
groll_zeileis.champion_pct_note: Top 8 (ESP..BRA) are the authors' exact published figures. Ranks 9-48 read off the published win-probability bar chart (p_win.png) and are approximate (+/- ~0.3pp); ordering is exact. Values for the bottom tail (~IRN downward) are sub-0.1% and shown as ~0.05 placeholders.

nate_silver.name: Nate Silver / Silver Bulletin PELE model
nate_silver.methodology: PELE ratings (successor to Silver's ESPN-era SPI) combining match results, player market values and detailed home-field adjustments; 100,000 tournament simulations.
nate_silver.champion_pct_note: Final published figures (June 9). Full 48-team table is paywalled; values shown are from Silver's own posts and the RealClearMarkets comparison piece. NOTE: an earlier June-5 snapshot had Argentina (18.7%) fractionally ahead of Spain (18.5%); Silver's final model docks several teams and makes Spain 'a reasonably clear #1' with ARG/ENG/FRA bunched for second. Norway and Senegal are notable PELE over-rates (Haaland/Mane market value).

opta.name: Opta / Stats Perform supercomputer
opta.methodology: Opta power-ranking team-strength model (recent form + historical international results) feeding a match-by-match betting-style simulation; 25,000 tournament simulations.
opta.champion_pct_note: Top teams are Opta's exact published figures. SUI/URU/JPN/SEN (~1.0-1.1%) and the listed sub-1% teams are from the article text; some mid/low-tier teams (TUR, CAN, CZE, etc.) were not individually quoted. Haiti won 1 of 25,000 sims; Curacao never won.

espn.name: ESPN — writer panel + DTAI/Leuven-informed predictions ('predicting every game')
espn.methodology: ESPN no longer publishes its own SPI; this is a human bracket by Ryan O'Hanlon anchored on DTAI (KU Leuven) Elo advance probabilities, plus a 19-writer poll for the awards. Champion %s below are the cited DTAI baseline, not ESPN's own model.
espn.champion_pct_note: Only ESP/FRA/ARG were given explicit percentages (from the DTAI/Leuven model O'Hanlon cites; 'no team better than 24%'). England/Germany/Brazil referenced but not numerically. Full DTAI table lives on the Leuven page (JS-rendered, not machine-readable).

champion_votes.note: Spain on every one of 19 ballots; France 7; ARG/POR/BRA/ENG appeared in top-4 picks

dtai_leuven.name: DTAI Sports Analytics Lab, KU Leuven
dtai_leuven.methodology: Elo-style team-strength rating from historical results feeding a Poisson match-outcome simulation; 20,000 tournament simulations, updated after each match.
dtai_leuven.champion_pct_note: Page title is literally 'Can Anyone Stop Spain?' (Spain clear favorite). The full 48-team table is a React/JS-rendered widget not extractable via fetch; ESP/FRA/ARG figures are those ESPN cited from this model. This is a stronger-favorite, lower-entropy model than Opta/Zeileis.

goldman_sachs.name: Goldman Sachs World Cup 2026 model
goldman_sachs.methodology: Elo-anchored Poisson goals model with a team 'mentality' adjustment; 50,000 Monte-Carlo tournament simulations.
goldman_sachs.champion_pct_note: Figures from the RealClearMarkets head-to-head with PELE; only these nine teams were quoted. Goldman is the most Spain-bullish / lowest-entropy model and applies a 'mentality' penalty that notably suppresses England (5.0%).

betting_market_consensus.name: Bookmaker consensus (de-vigged), for comparison — sourced from project odds-market.json
betting_market_consensus.note: De-vigged implied probabilities (DraftKings/consensus). Market makes France a near co-favorite with Spain and rates Portugal & Brazil clearly higher than the analytical models do.

consensus_method: Unweighted mean across the DISTINCT model families for each team, using only models that quoted a number for that team. To avoid double-counting, ESPN's champion %s (which it borrows from DTAI/Leuven) are collapsed into dtai_leuven, leaving FIVE distinct model families in the average: groll_zeileis, nate_silver (PELE), opta, dtai_leuven, goldman_sachs. Betting market is NOT included (kept separate as the market benchmark). Caveat: top teams (ESP..NOR) are averaged over 4-5 models and are robust; teams quoted by only 1-2 models (BEL down to SWE, and the entire <0.8% tail) lean heavily on Zeileis/Opta and should be treated as indicative only. The mean is pulled UP for top teams by the two very top-heavy Elo models (Goldman, DTAI), so the consensus ESP 19.3% sits above the median model (~16%).

model_agreement_summary.champion_pick: Unanimous: ALL six models pick SPAIN as most likely champion.
model_agreement_summary.second_tier: France is #2 in 4 of 6 models (Opta, Zeileis ties ENG, Goldman, market). Argentina is #2 in PELE & DTAI/ESPN. England, France, Argentina form a tight bunch for 2nd-4th.
key_disagreements: Entropy/favorite-strength: Goldman (ESP 25.7%) and DTAI/ESPN (ESP 24%) are very top-heavy; Groll-Zeileis (ESP 14.5%) and Opta (16.1%) are flat / wide-open. PELE in between (16.1%).
England: Goldman docks England hard (5.0%) via a 'mentality' factor; Zeileis (12.4%), Opta (11.2%) and PELE (10.4%) rate them a genuine top-4 side.
Norway: Opta (3.5%) and especially PELE (4.0%) rate Norway as a real dark horse (Haaland); Goldman (1.6%) much cooler. Norway out-rates several traditional powers in PELE.
Germany: Zeileis loves Germany (11.2%, ~4th); Opta (5.1%), PELE (6.6%) and Goldman (4.5%) have them mid-pack.
Senegal: PELE boosts Senegal (Mane market value) well above where Elo-only models place them.

model_vs_market_disagreements: FRANCE: market makes France a near co-favorite with Spain (14.8% devig, +475), but most analytical models rate France clearly below Spain (Zeileis 12.4%, PELE 11.7%, Opta 13.0%). PELE explicitly docks France for its brutal group (Norway + Senegal) to 11.7% vs the market's ~15-17%.
PORTUGAL: market (9.0%, +850, 4th-shortest price) is more bullish than the models — Opta 7.0%, Zeileis 8.9%, PELE 4.9%, Goldman 4.8%. PELE/Goldman see Portugal as overpriced by the book.
BRAZIL: market (8.1%, +950) rates Brazil above where Zeileis (4.7%), Goldman (7.6%) and PELE (6.1%) put them; models are more skeptical of Brazil than the market.
ARGENTINA: market relatively cool (8.5%, +900) vs PELE (11.0%), DTAI/ESPN (17%) and Goldman (14.3%) — analytical models like the holders more than the book does.
SPAIN: Goldman (25.7%) and DTAI (24%) price Spain far above the market's ~15.5%; Opta/PELE/Zeileis sit much closer to the market.
ENGLAND: market (10.6%) sits with the analytical mainstream (~10-12%); only Goldman (5.0%) is the outlier-low view.
notes: Today is 2026-06-10; figures are the most recent published as of this date. (1) The Conversation '100,000 simulations' piece is CONFIRMED to be the Groll/Zeileis forecast (same authors, same 100k sims, identical top-8 figures) — recorded under groll_zeileis, not as a separate model. (2) ESPN no longer runs its own SPI model; the old FiveThirtyEight SPI was retired and its lineage now lives in Nate Silver's PELE — so 'ESPN SPI' and 'FiveThirtyEight-style' are effectively the PELE entry plus ESPN's writer panel. (3) Groll/Zeileis publish full per-team numbers only in an interactive graphic / PNG bar chart; ranks 9-48 here are read off that chart and are approximate (ordering exact, magnitudes +/- ~0.3pp). (4) DTAI Leuven and the DTAI-derived ESPN numbers share the same underlying model, hence near-identical figures. (5) Nate Silver PELE moved between June 5 (ARG fractionally ahead) and June 9 (ESP clear #1) — the June 9 finalized version is used. (6) No model published an explicit single predicted top scorer except ESPN's writer poll (Mbappe). Betting-market consensus is included for comparison but excluded from consensus_champion_pct.
